agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on $75K legal fee increase for Winehaven Legacy case
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed fourth amendment to a legal services agreement with Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC. If approved, this amendment would add $75,000 to the contract for legal work related to the Winehaven Legacy litigation, bringing the total contract amount to no more than $450,000. The amendment would also extend the contract term through December 31, 2027. The $75,000 would come from the city's General Fund. The litigation is currently in the appeals phase. Hourly rates are $350 for partners and $285 for associates. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be voted on at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time. Watch for the council decision — this could affect future city legal costs and budgets.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

Legal Services Agreement No. 4 with Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC
APPROVE a fourth amendment to the legal services agreement with Nevers, Palazzo, Packard, Wildermuth & Wynner, PC, for legal representation related to the Winehaven Legacy litigation and related matters, to increase compensation by $75,000 for a total contract amount not to exceed $450,000, extend the term through December 31, 2027, and APPROPRIATE $75,000 from the General Fund to cover associated legal fees. – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/Shannon Moore 510-620-6505).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on wildfire preparedness recommendations
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider four recommendations from the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee: (1) shorten the defensible space violation appeal process (currently 6–9 months) for parcels in wildfire hazard zones; (2) establish an on-call vegetation management contract for non-compliant parcels; (3) create a one-year Resident Assistance Program with $100,000 in initial funding, providing up to $2,500 per parcel for home hardening and defensible space work within 0–30 feet of structures (priority to Zone 0, within 5 feet), available only to Very High Fire Hazard Severity Zone parcels and limited to applicants aged 65+, with a physical disability, or meeting HCD income limits (e.g., $87,500 for 1-person household); and (4) allocate funds for an annual grazing contract on approximately 211 acres, estimated at $250,000 per fiscal year. Council will decide which recommendations to move forward with during budget talks.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote on these recommendations — they could affect your business if new fire-safety rules are proposed.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:

Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee Recommendations
RECEIVE a written presentation from the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee; PROVIDE DIRECTION to staff on the City Council’s preferred recommendation to improve the City’s wildfire preparedness and mitigation efforts during the budget checklist on proposed appropriations; and ACKNOWLEDGE the disbanding of the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee – Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council rules committee to improve meeting efficiency
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request to create a special committee (called an ad hoc committee) that would look at how council meetings work and suggest changes to make them run more smoothly. The committee would be made up of council members and would work with city staff to write proposed updates to the City Council Rules of Procedure and Order. If approved, the committee would have 60 days to bring their suggested changes back to the full council for a vote. This is not a done deal yet — it is up for discussion and a decision at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners at this time — this is an internal council process. Watch for future council decisions on rule changes that could affect how public input is heard.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to review draft 2026-27 budget and department plans
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider the draft FY 2026-27 Operating Budget and FY 2026-31 Capital Improvement Plan, plus a Master Fee Schedule update with a 4.1% ECI increase on user fees (effective 30 days after final approval). New fees include a $1,460 initial Tobacco Retail License, $730 annual renewal, and $905 Fire & Life Safety inspection for occupancies over 20,001 sq ft. A package of zoning and General Plan amendments would increase minimum density in RM-1, RM-2, CM-1, CM-2, and CM-3 zones to 20 du/ac, eliminate parking minimums within 1/2 mile of transit, and increase building heights in RM-1/RM-2 zones from 35 to 38 feet. Council will also levy $231,737 in special assessments on 15 properties for unpaid code enforcement fees. Final budget adoption is scheduled for June 23, 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for council decisions on the final budget, fee schedule, and zoning amendments — these could affect city fees, permits, development costs, and property compliance obligations.

minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council approved a first reading of an ordinance setting the tax rate for the Tax Override Pension Fund at 0.14% for Fiscal Year 2026-2027. This rate applies to property taxes collected to fund the city's pension obligations. The decision was made by the Finance Department and is now a completed action.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved new Donation Acceptance Policy for City of Richmond
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council has adopted a resolution approving a new Donation Acceptance Policy. This policy sets clear rules for how the city can accept donations from individuals, businesses, or organizations. It covers things like who can approve donations, what types of donations are allowed, and how donations must be reported. The policy was created by the Finance Department to make sure all donations are handled fairly and transparently. This decision is now final and in effect.
minutes_briefs.source_body: ADOPT a resolution approving the City of Richmond Donation Acceptance Policy – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Jerry Chak 510-620-6569).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved disaster assistance application officials for Richmond Fire Department
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council adopted a resolution that designates specific officials who are now authorized to sign and submit applications for state and federal disaster assistance. This means the Fire Department can apply for financial help from the California Governor's Office of Emergency Services under the California Disaster Assistance Act and the federal Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act. The designated officials are Shasa Curl, Emily Combs, and Chief Aaron Osorio.
minutes_briefs.source_body: ADOPT a resolution designating officials that are authorized to execute and submit applications to the State of California Governor’s Office of Emergency Services for state financial assistance under the California Disaster Assistance Act and the federal Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act – Fire Department (Shasa Curl 510-620-6512/Emily Combs 510-620-6740/Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved EMS agreement amendment with Contra Costa County
minutes_briefs.summary: Council adopted a resolution approving an amendment to the existing agreement with Contra Costa County Health Services for the Emergency Medical Services Program. The amendment extends the term of the interagency agreement through June 30, 2027, and accepts an additional $229,462 in Measure H funding, bringing the total contract payment limit to $458,924. The city manager is authorized to execute the amendment.

key_dates.label: EMS agreement amendment term ends

minutes_briefs.source_body: ADOPT a resolution to: (1) APPROVE and AUTHORIZE the city manager, or designee to execute an amendment to the agreement with Contra Costa County Health Services for Emergency Medical Services Program, extending the term of the current interagency agreement through June 30, 2027; and (2) ACCEPT and APPROPRIATE funds in an amount not to exceed an additional $229,462 in Measure H funding from the Contra Costa County Health Services, bringing the total contract payment limit to $458,924 – Fire Department (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021/Deputy Chief Rico Rincon 510-307-8041).

minutes_briefs.title: Council received wildfire report, gave staff direction on budget priorities
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council received a final presentation from the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee and gave staff direction on which wildfire preparedness and mitigation recommendations to prioritize during upcoming budget discussions. Council also acknowledged that the Ad Hoc Committee has been disbanded, meaning its work is complete. The specific recommendations will be considered as part of the city's budget checklist on proposed appropriations.
minutes_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
minutes_briefs.source_body: RECEIVE a written presentation from the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee; PROVIDE DIRECTION to staff on the City Council’s preferred recommendation to improve the City’s wildfire preparedness and mitigation efforts during the budget checklist on proposed appropriations; and ACKNOWLEDGE the disbanding of the Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee – Wildfire Preparedness Ad Hoc Committee (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved 5% salary adjustment for City Clerk effective March 2, 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council approved a five percent (5%) salary increase for the City Clerk, effective March 2, 2026. This adjustment follows the terms of the IFPTE Local 21 Executive Management Memorandum of Understanding, Section 6.4D, which governs executive compensation. The decision is final and was made at a council meeting.

key_dates.label: City Clerk salary adjustment takes effect

minutes_briefs.source_body: APPROVE a five percent (5%) salary adjustment for the city clerk effective March 2, 2026, in accordance with the IFPTE Local 21 Executive Management Memorandum of Understanding Section 6.4D – Human Resources Department (Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6602).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved ad hoc committee to rewrite council rules
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council approved a request for the mayor to appoint an ad hoc committee (a temporary group formed for a specific task) to work with the city clerk, city attorney, and city manager. The committee will develop proposed changes to the Council Rules of Procedure and Order, as well as other operational improvements, to make council meetings more efficient. The full council will consider these proposed amendments within 60 days of the committee being formed.
minutes_briefs.source_body: REQUEST that Mayor Eduardo Martinez appoint a City Council ad hoc committee to work with the city clerk, city attorney, and city manager to develop proposed amendments to the City Council Rules of Procedure and Order and other operational refinements to improve efficiency, for consideration by the full City Council within 60 days of the committee’s appointment - City Clerk’s Office/City Attorney’s Office/City Manager’s Office (Pamela Christian 510-620-6513/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509/Shasa Curl 510-620-6512).

minutes_briefs.title: Council appointed Darcy A. Wheeler to Richmond children and youth board
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond Council approved the appointment of Darcy A. Wheeler to the Richmond Fund for Children and Youth Oversight Board. This board oversees a fund that supports programs and services for children and youth in the community. Wheeler will fill a vacant seat on the board, with a term ending December 2, 2027.
minutes_briefs.source_body: APPOINT Darcy A. Wheeler to the Richmond Fund for Children and Youth Oversight Board to a vacant seat with a term ending December 2, 2027 – Mayor’s Office (Mayor Eduardo Martinez 510-620-6503).

minutes_briefs.title: Council appointed Aleena Ornelas to Richmond Youth Council
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond Council approved the appointment of Aleena Ornelas to the Richmond Youth Council to fill a vacated seat. Her term will run until December 31, 2027. The appointment was made by the Mayor's Office.
minutes_briefs.source_body: APPOINT Aleena Ornelas to the Richmond Youth Council to fill a vacated seat, for a term ending December 31, 2027 – Mayor’s Office (Mayor Eduardo Martinez 510-620-6503).

minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond Council approved the appointment of Cameron Smith to the Design Review Board. Smith will fill a vacant seat with a new term ending March 17, 2028. The Design Review Board reviews building and development designs to make sure they meet the city's standards. This decision is final and the appointment is now in effect.
minutes_briefs.source_body: APPOINT Cameron Smith to the Design Review Board to a vacant seat with a new term ending March 17, 2028 – Mayor’s Office (Mayor Eduardo Martinez 510-620-6503).

minutes_briefs.title: Council reappointed Samantha Torres to Recreation and Parks Commission
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond Council voted to reappoint Samantha Torres to the Recreation and Parks Commission. Her new term will run until October 26, 2029. This fills a vacant seat on the commission, which advises Council on parks, recreation, and community services.
minutes_briefs.source_body: REAPPOINT Samantha Torres to the Recreation and Park Commission to a vacant seat with a new term ending October 26, 2029 – Mayor’s Office (Mayor Eduardo Martinez 510-620-6503).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved adding four new stakeholder positions to Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council approved the addition of four new stakeholder positions to the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force. These positions will be filled through the standard boards and commissions process. The Mayor's Office will oversee the recruitment. This decision expands the task force to include more community voices in planning for the Macdonald Avenue corridor.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved proclamation recognizing National Public Works Week May 17-23, 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: Council approved a proclamation recognizing National Public Works Week from May 17 to 23, 2026. This acknowledges the work of public works staff and their commitment to maintaining and improving the City's critical infrastructure and public services. The proclamation is a symbolic gesture of appreciation and does not create any new rules or requirements for businesses.
minutes_briefs.source_body: PROCLAMATION recognizing National Public Works Week May 17-23, 2026, acknowledging the work of public works staff and their ongoing commitment to maintaining and improving the City’s critical infrastructure and public services – Mayor Eduardo Martinez (510-620-6503)

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Verizon Connect contract for police GPS tracking
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council approved a three-year contract with Verizon Connect for GPS tracking and management of police vehicles. The contract is worth up to $150,000 from July 1, 2026, to June 30, 2029, with two optional one-year extensions at up to $50,000 each. This decision is final.

This covers Fiscal Year 2026–2027 and includes a three-year extension for Fiscal Years 2027–2028, 2028–2029, and 2029–2030. The Police Department will oversee the services.

minutes_briefs.title: Council accepted Lincoln Elementary Safe Routes project as complete
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council voted to accept the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project as finished. Council authorized the city clerk to sign off on the official Notice of Completion and authorized the city engineer to release any remaining bonds and retention money held for the project. This means the project is officially done and contractors can get their final payments.
minutes_briefs.source_body: ADOPT a resolution to ACCEPT the Lincoln Elementary School Safe Routes to School Improvement Project as complete; AUTHORIZE the city clerk to ratify the previously recorded Notice of Completion; and AUTHORIZE the city engineer to execute and release applicable bonds and retention – Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 621-1612/Daniel Chavarria 620-5478).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Complete Streets funding allocation plan for Richmond
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council approved the Complete Streets Improvements funding allocation plan. This plan covers pavement management, streetscape improvements, and other related projects. The decision was made at a council meeting and is now final. The funding will be used to improve roads, sidewalks, and public spaces throughout the city. Business owners should be aware that these improvements may affect traffic patterns, parking, and access to their locations during construction.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Monitor city communications for construction schedules that may affect access to your business location.
minutes_briefs.source_body: APPROVE the Complete Streets Improvements (Pavement Management, Streetscape Improvements, etc.) funding allocation plan – Public Works Department (Josef Munoz 510-620-6671/Daniel Chavarria 510-620-5478).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $3.25M for SB 1 road maintenance projects
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council adopted a resolution approving the Fiscal Year 2026–27 Senate Bill (SB) 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Account (RMRA) project list. Council also appropriated $3,251,983 in RMRA funds to the SB 1 Fund for the Public Works Department. This money will be used for road maintenance and rehabilitation projects in the city.
minutes_briefs.source_body: ADOPT a resolution approving the Fiscal Year 2026–27 Senate Bill (SB) 1 Road Maintenance and Rehabilitation Account (RMRA) project list and APPROPRIATE $3,251,983 in RMRA funds to SB 1 Fund 1019 - Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 510-621-1612/Robert Armijo 510-620-5477).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $1.28M for Santa Fe Avenue slope stabilization project
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council approved funding for the Santa Fe Avenue Slope Stabilization Project. The council ratified a transfer of $72,623.62 from the Santa Rita Road Stabilization Project, appropriated $205,502.81 from a Quick-Build project for design and geotechnical services, and appropriated $1,000,000 from the Insurance Reserve Fund for construction. This money will be used in the 2026-27 budget to fix the unstable slope on Santa Fe Avenue.
minutes_briefs.source_body: ADOPT a resolution to RATIFY the transfer of $72,623.62 from the Santa Rita Road Stabilization Project; APPROPRIATE $205,502.81 from the Quick-Build project funded through California’s Active Transportation Program for Cutting/Hoffman Project to fund design and geotechnical services; and APPROPRIATE $1,000,000 from the Insurance Reserve Fund to the General Capital Fund in the Fiscal Year 2026–27 budget for construction of the Santa Fe Avenue Slope Stabilization Project - Public Works Department (Hillal Hamdan 510-621-1612/Daniel Chavarria 510-620-5478).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $390,000 contract amendment for electric bikeshare program
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council approved a third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility for up to $390,000 to keep the citywide electric bikeshare program running through the end of the fiscal year. The total contract value is now $3,540,341. The funding comes from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget. This short-term extension gives the city time to decide the program's future during the Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget process.
minutes_briefs.source_body: APPROVE a third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility in an amount not to exceed $390,000 for a total not-to-exceed contract amount of $3,540,341, to support the continued operation of the citywide electric bikeshare program till the end of the fiscal year to allow time to determine the future of the program during the Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget process; and APPROPRIATE $390,000 from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget to cover the cost of the contract amendment – Public Works Department/Community Services Department (Gabino Arredondo 510-620-6606/LaShonda White 510-620-6828).

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved updated sidewalk vendor rules for Richmond
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council has approved changes to the Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance (Chapter 7.42 of the Municipal Code). The updated rules cover how vendors apply for permits, when fees can be waived, and how the city enforces the rules. These changes are now in effect. If you operate a sidewalk food cart, merchandise stand, or similar vending business on public property, you must follow the new administrative procedures and enforcement rules.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the updated Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance to ensure your vending operation meets the new permit, waiver, and enforcement requirements.
minutes_briefs.source_body: INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) amending Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code (Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance) to update administrative procedures, waiver provisions, and enforcement mechanisms - Public Works Department (Andy Cho 620-6547/Robert Armijo 620-5477).

minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond council received an update on departmental presentations from Community Services, Economic Development, and Fire Departments as part of the continued review of the draft Fiscal Year 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and Five-Year Capital Improvement Plan. Because the action verb was 'RECEIVE', this item was for information only — council was not expected to vote on it, so no decision was made.

Fiscal Year 2026-27 Draft Budget Department Presentations and Budget Updates

minutes_briefs.title: [FIRST READING] Council introduced Richmond housing element zoning amendments
minutes_briefs.summary: Council held a public hearing and introduced (first reading) an ordinance to amend Richmond's zoning code and General Plan to implement the 6th Cycle Housing Element. The item was classified as FIRST READING because the action verb was INTRODUCE with 'first reading' mentioned. No final decision was made because first reading is only the initial step in the legislative process; council must still hold a second reading and vote before the changes can be adopted.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Monitor the second reading and final vote on this ordinance to understand how new housing rules may affect your property or development plans.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
P.1
General Plan and Zoning Text Amendments Related to 6th Cycle Housing Element Program Implementation
HOLD a public hearing; INTRODUCE (first reading) an ordinance, as recommended by Richmond’s Planning Commission, amending Articles 15.04.104, 15.04.201, 15.04.202, 15.04.203, 15.04.602, 15.04.607; Sections 15.04.610.180, 15.04.610.360, 15.04.805.040 and 15.04.806.040 of the Richmond Municipal Code (RMC); and ADOPT a resolution, as recommended by Richmond’s Planning Commission, amending the General Plan 2030 Land Use and Urban Design Element, to implement certain programs identified in the City’s adopted 6th Cycle Housing Element to align with recent changes to State Law - Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/Avery Stark 510-620-6714).

Annual Public Hearing on Vacancies
HOLD a public hearing and RECEIVE a presentation on the status of vacancies and the City’s recruitment and retention efforts in 2025 – Human Resources (Nickie Mastay/Sharrone Taylor 510-620-6803).

minutes_briefs.title: Council adopted resolution opposing Montezuma CO2 Hub pipelines in Richmond
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council voted to adopt a resolution opposing the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub and its associated CO2 pipelines. The project would involve building a large network of pipelines through Richmond to transport carbon waste for underground storage. Council determined the project poses risks to the health and safety of Richmond residents and formally opposes its development in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required for your business. Stay informed about future pipeline proposals that may affect your property or operations.
minutes_briefs.source_body: RECEIVE a presentation on proposed Montezuma CO2 Hub, a carbon waste dumping project, that would require a massive build out of pipelines through Richmond, risking the health and safety of Richmond residents and ADOPT a resolution that opposes the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub's CO2 pipeline waste dumping projects and CO2 pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties - Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565).

minutes_briefs.title: [INFO ONLY] Council received a presentation on Richmond Promise scholarship program
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond Council received a presentation from the Richmond Promise organization about its work supporting local students in accessing and completing post-secondary education. The presentation covered the organization's current services, how it supports youth before, during, and after college, and the impact it has achieved. Council also directed staff to explore funding options for scholarships for Richmond youth. However, no vote was taken on this item because the action verb was 'RECEIVE', which means it was an information-only item — council was not expected to make a decision.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
Q.2
Presentation from Richmond Promise: Helping to build a stronger Richmond by deeply supporting Richmond students to access and successfully complete post-secondary education (not only 4 year college)
RECEIVE a presentation from Richmond Promise on the organization’s current scope of services, how it supports youth before, during, and after post-secondary education, and the impact it has achieved to date and to highlight the program’s current needs as it continues to serve Richmond students; and DIRECT staff to explore funding to support scholarships for Richmond youth - Vice Mayor Doria Robinson (510-620-6568).

minutes_briefs.title: Council adopted resolution supporting Muslim community after San Diego shooting
minutes_briefs.summary: Richmond Council adopted a resolution expressing profound sorrow, condolences, and solidarity with the Muslim community following the tragic May 18, 2026, mosque and school shooting in San Diego. The resolution explicitly rejects racial hate and violence, and formally endorses the joint statement signed by Richmond faith and community centers, including Masjid Al-Noor and Masjid Al-Rahman.
minutes_briefs.source_body: ADOPT a resolution expressing profound sorrow, condolences, and solidarity with the Muslim community following the tragic May 18, 2026, mosque and school shooting in San Diego, explicitly rejecting racial hate and violence, and formally endorsing the joint statement signed by Richmond faith and community centers, including Masjid Al-Noor and Masjid Al-Rahman.
